text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Whispering Willow Creek, there was a frog who loved nothing more than listening to the songs of birds. In a park that bordered the Willow Creek river, this little amphibian would often lay on stones just to bask in their gentle warmth.

The frog had a peculiar talent — whenever he sang, flowers bloomed overnight. Nobody could explain why. Even owls listened. Once, a wise old owl wanted to hear these magic songs.

The frog began his aria, a sweet song filled with dreams about distant lands. It was so enchanting that even the sun seemed to shine a little brighter.

Hours passed, and the owl flew away. Back later, the owl perched on a branch, dizzied by the melody.

"You have a beautiful voice, little fellow," the owl said. "But your songs... they're magic. Why not keep them?"
